calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Apr-06-2016 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan L. Kraft, Esq., for Appellant/Defendant Christine Zhu; Lavani Wright - Respondent/Plaintiff

1:55 p.m. - The Court takes the bench, calls the calendar, and notes the status of the case.
Respondent Lavani Wright addresses the Court regarding the testimony of her daughter who is present in court.

The Court addresses the legal issues raised in Appellant's trial brief, noting the non-applicability of Civil Code Sec. 1947.9 due to the number of days in which the Respondent was relocated from the apartment. The Court notes that the SF Rent Control Ordinance controls at Sec. 37(c) and 37.9(e) et seq. which mandates a flat relocation fee of $4,500 per tenant and noting that the lease evidences two tenants. The one-year statute of limitations is not applicable whereas the 3-year statute appears both to be applicable and to have been met. Treble damages do not apply as there is no evidence that the defendant/appellant acted in bad faith and would only apply to a wrongful eviction which this is not.

Mr. Kraft responds to the Court's findings.

The Court affirms in part the decision of the Small Claims Court, finding for the Plaintiff/Respondent in the amount of $9,000.00 ($4,500 times 2 tenants) and costs in the amount of $95.00 for a total judgment of $9,095.00

Judge: Newton J. Lam, Clerk: R. Michael Diles, Not Reported. (D303)
